news.headline: Dan Bongino, a content creator, appointed as Deputy Director of the FBI in the Trump Administration.
news.summary: A statement was released by Rumble (NASDAQ:RUM) on Feb. 23, 2025, in Longboat Key, Fla., announcing the appointment of content creator Dan Bongino as Deputy Director of the FBI in the Trump Administration. The statement was issued by CEO and Chairman Chris Pavlovski. Rumble is a video-sharing platform and cloud services provider.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency scammers are exploiting AI bots, using fake tokens with backdoors, hurting automated traders.
news.summary: Criminals are changing tactics in the AI bot battle, utilizing counterfeit tokens with concealed vulnerabilities to take advantage of the unregulated nature of the cryptocurrency market.

news.summary: Euro made significant gains in the market following an election win by German conservatives. The currency surged by a notable percentage, reflecting investor confidence in the outcome. The news comes from Bloomberg's Asia Trade report dated 2/21/25.

news.summary: Shortly after the news broke that Bybit had acquired $742 million worth of Ether between February 22 and 23, a statement was issued by the company.

news.summary: Markus Thielen from 10x Research suggests that the interest in Bitcoin as a long-term investment may not be as large as reported in the media.

news.headline: Faraday Future launches FF Global AI Innovation Competition for shaping intelligent mobility solutions. (80)
news.summary: In Los Angeles, Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIE) has announced the start of their FF Global AI Innovation Competition. The competition is open to all individuals interested in AI technology to revolutionize intelligent mobility. Faraday Future, a California-based global company, is leading the way in AI-powered automotive technology. The initiative aims to redefine the future of intelligent mobility through innovative AI-driven solutions.

news.headline: Trump, Macron to participate in a G7 leader summit call 8am ET Monday
Trump to hold a press conference with Macron at 2pm ET on Monday
Bybit recovers from crypto heist, freezes $42.89M of stolen funds, secures $1.23B in ETH with help from various parties.
news.summary: Bybit managed to freeze $42.89 million of the stolen funds within a day following the massive $1.4 billion hack incident. This development came after hackers illicitly obtained over $1.4 billion worth of Ethereum and related tokens. The successful freezing of the funds was achieved through a joint endeavor involving various parties such as Tether, Circle, Avalanche ecosystem, and Bitget. The involvement of stablecoin issuers like Tether and entities from the cryptocurrency exchange Bitget played a crucial role in this operation. The coordinated effort effectively prevented the hackers from moving the frozen funds, significantly boosting Bybit's reserve security in the aftermath of the cyber attack.

news.headline: Bybit invests $1M in EthicHub via BGA to drive agricultural finance for global farming communities. World's 2nd largest exchange.
news.summary: In Dubai on February 23, 2025, Bybit, the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange in terms of trading volume, revealed its $1 million impact investment in EthicHub via the Blockchain for Good Alliance (BGA). This investment marks a significant step in Bybit's commitment to promoting positive blockchain applications.

news.summary: An agreement signed on February 23, 2025, in São Paulo aims to enhance access to advanced genomic technologies and enhance diagnostic accuracy. MGI Tech Co., Ltd. ("MGI"), dedicated to developing key tools in the life sciences sector, is involved in this initiative. The agreement is set to significantly impact the industry and facilitate the adoption of groundbreaking genomic solutions. This collaboration is expected to benefit numerous individuals and organizations, unlocking the potential of cutting-edge technologies in the field. The agreement is part of efforts to advance research and innovation, potentially improving outcomes for patients and researchers alike.

Domino's Q4 earnings forecast: $4.91 per share, revenue of $1.48 billion, shares fell 2% to $462.37.
news.summary: Ann Arbor-based Domino’s Pizza, Inc. is set to publish its Q4 financial results on Feb. 24, 2025. Analysts predict earnings of $4.91 per share, up from $4.48 in the previous year. Anticipated revenue is $1.48 billion, an increase from $1.4 billion. In the last report on Oct. 10, sales stood at $1.08 billion, falling short of the expected $1.10 billion. However, EPS was $4.19, surpassing the estimate of $3.68. The company’s shares decreased by 2% to $462.37 upon closing on Friday. For more details, refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Government departments advise employees to delay response to Musk's ultimatum.

Lucara Diamond Corp's 2024 results reveal record production with 399,215 carats sold for revenue of $203.9M. Notable recoveries include 2,488 carat Motswedi and 1,094 carat Seriti diamonds alongside operational progress. The Karowe Mine achieved new benchmarks with 2.9M tonnes of ore processed, setting a new production record. Operating margins rose to 61% versus 58% in 2023. The cash position as of Dec 31, 2024, stood at $22.8M, with $180M drawn from the $190M Project Facility for the Karowe UGP. Diamond market outlook shows a potential increase in demand for larger diamonds despite challenges. Karowe diamonds are sold via multiple channels including HB sales and quarterly tenders. The company spent $64.7M on the UGP development in 2024. The UGP progress includes reaching certain milestones in 2024 and an update to the production commencement schedule.
news.summary: Lucara Diamond Corp. reported its results for the year ended December 31, 2024, highlighting operational achievements and financial performance. Karowe operated for over three years without lost time injury, recovering exceptional diamonds like the 2,488 carat Motswedi and 1,094 carat Seriti. Sales totaled 399,215 carats, amounting to $203.9 million. Operating margins were 61%, with a cost per tonne processed of $27.14. The company had $22.8 million in cash and $27.1 million in working capital by the end of 2024. The Karowe Mine saw record production with 2.9 Mt of ore processed. The Karowe Underground Project (UGP) is expected to commence production in H1 2028 with a forecasted cost of $683.4 million. Lucara sold Clara Diamond Solutions for $3.0 million in cash and 10,000,000 common shares. The diamond market shows signs of recovery with anticipated demand for larger diamonds. The UGP achieved a 0.54 Total Recordable Injury Frequency Rate. Planned activities for 2025 include sinking shafts to final depths and procurement of underground equipment.

news.headline: Metal company to raise funds through private placement and collaborates with market maker for trades.
news.summary: In its latest report, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) revised its global growth forecast for the year to 4.9%, a 0.5% decrease from its previous projection. The IMF cited the ongoing effects of the COVID-19 pandemic as the primary reason for the downgrade. Advanced economies are expected to see a 5.8% growth, while emerging markets and developing economies are projected to grow by 6.7%. The IMF continues to stress the importance of policy support to ensure a robust economic recovery.

news.headline: A method allegedly predicts S&P 500 with 85% accuracy by focusing on global liquidity, not just U.S. factors.
news.summary: The article discusses a method to predict the S&P 500 with an approximately 85% correlation by focusing on global liquidity rather than just U.S. liquidity indicators. While investors typically consider U.S. CPI, interest rates, and M2 supply, the article argues that global market dynamics play a crucial role in S&P 500 movements. Global investors tend to invest in major U.S. companies like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, leading to significant foreign capital flowing into the S&P 500. The article emphasizes the importance of global liquidity, which is challenging to measure accurately. For the full story, readers are directed to Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Hampton debentures fully redeemed and reinvested.
news.summary: A new report revealed that in the previous year, approximately 3 million trees were planted across the country as part of a nationwide reforestation effort. The initiative aimed to address deforestation and combat climate change. The project received funding of $5 million from various government sources and private donors. The report highlighted that the newly planted trees are expected to absorb an estimated 500,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide annually, contributing significantly to the country's environmental goals. Furthermore, the reforestation effort created over 2,000 jobs in rural communities, providing economic benefits while also promoting sustainability.Officials expressed optimism about the positive impact of the project on both the environment and the local economy.

news.headline: Meta Platforms, Inc. doubled executive bonuses amidst 3,600 layoffs; bonus target raised from 75% to 200% of base salary. CEO excluded.
news.summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) has significantly increased its bonus plan for executives amidst recent layoffs that affected around 3,600 employees. The company approved a new bonus structure on Feb. 13, detailed in an SEC filing, which boosts the annual target bonus amount from 75% to 200% of each named executive officer's base salary. Notably, CEO Mark Zuckerberg is excluded from this bonus structure. This decision follows the recent layoffs and has raised concerns about the company's priorities and allocation of resources. For more information, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Ungate and EigenLayer team up to create a cryptographic trust layer for AI, redefining the future of blockchain tech.
news.summary: Ungate Wukong and EigenLayer have developed a groundbreaking trust layer for artificial intelligence. This innovation allows for the cryptographic verification of self-governing entities through decentralized validation. This initiative is poised to revolutionize the intersection of AI and blockchain technology, setting a new standard for trust and security in this rapidly evolving field.

news.headline: Altcoins surge, SEC to drop Coinbase suit, KAITO & IP soar! BTC nears $100k, ETH outperforms. 1m BTC reserves may offset $21T debt. US to buy 4.2m BTC. IP rises 150%, Sonic DEX volume up 85%, S up 60%. Africa company buys $10m BTC. LTC ETF on DTCC. SBF seeks Trump pardon. SEC approves yield-bearing stablecoins.
news.summary: Bitcoin is edging closer to the $100,000 mark, while Ethereum continues to showcase strong performance in the market. As meme activity wanes, altcoins are beginning to gain traction. The SEC is reportedly planning to drop a lawsuit against Coinbase. VanEck suggests that a reserve of 1 million BTC could potentially counterbalance the staggering $21 trillion debt. MicroStrategy CEO Michael Saylor announces intentions for the US government to acquire 4.2 million BTC for reserves. A bill in Arizona concerning a BTC reserve is making its way to the Senate, while a similar bill in Utah is scheduled for voting in May. Initial Public Offerings have surged by 150% this week, with a particular focus on new altcoins. Sonic, a weekly decentralized exchange, experiences an 85% increase in volume, with S seeing a 60% hike. Some countries are facing restrictions on payouts from FTX. The first publicly traded African company makes a $10 million investment in BTC. A Litecoin ETF is now listed on the Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation. Sam Bankman-Fried, the CEO of FTX, seeks a pardon from former US President Donald Trump. The SEC grants approval for stablecoins that generate yield.

news.headline: eXch denies laundering $35M from Bybit hack as claimed by ZachXBT & Nick Bax; linked to Lazarus Group.
news.summary: ZachXBT, an expert in blockchain analysis, and Nick Bax, a member of an ethical hacker group, claim that the Lazarus Group utilized eXch to launder a significant sum of $35 million allegedly obtained from the Bybit hack.

news.headline: Bitcoin and Dogecoin struggle after Bybit's billion-dollar hack, Ethereum gains. BTC: -0.24%, $96,377.59ETH: +2.66%, $2,832.54DOGE: -1.13%, $0.2435. Total crypto liquidations hit $131.20M in 24 hours.
news.summary: On Sunday, leading cryptocurrencies were weak due to a $1.4 billion hack on Bybit exchange. Bitcoin was at $96,377.59, down 0.24%, struggling to break $100,000. Ethereum, at $2,832.54, rose 2.66% after being at the center of the breach. Dogecoin fell 1.13% to $0.2435. Liquidations reached $131.20 million, wiping out $79 million in bets. Bitcoin's interest rose 0.75%, with over 50% of traders short. The market sentiment was neutral. Top gainers were Sonic (+6.98%), Helium (+6.12%), and Injective (+5.45%). The global crypto market cap was $3.19 trillion, down 0.27% in 24 hours.

news.headline: Global Electric Motors Market set to reach $206.4 billion by 2029, growing at 6.3% CAGR. Rising demand in HVAC, manufacturing, and energy-efficient sectors drives the market. EV industry boosts demand for electric motors. EVs are key revenue generators.
news.summary: The global Electric Motors Market is expected to grow from USD 152.2 billion in 2024 to USD 206.4 billion by 2029, at a CAGR of 6.3%. Factors driving this growth include the rising demand for HVAC systems in various sectors, the increasing use of electric motors in manufacturing industries, and the emphasis on energy-efficient motors. The surge in the electric vehicle (EV) industry is also contributing to this growth, as electric motors are essential components of EVs. The production and adoption of EVs directly influence the demand for electric motors, making the EV industry a significant revenue generator for motor manufacturers. The full report is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Peter Schiff criticizes Fed's 2% inflation target, calling it a pretext for monetary policy. Target was zero for first 99 years. Fed started targeting 2% inflation in 2012 with a concept from New Zealand in 1995. Fed Chair Powell raises persistent inflation concerns.
news.summary: Economist Peter Schiff criticized the Federal Reserve's 2% inflation target, claiming it is used to justify accommodative monetary policy. This comes after Fed Chair Jerome Powell voiced concerns about persistent inflation during a recent congressional testimony. Schiff argued that the 2% target, implemented in 2012, has enabled quantitative easing and zero interest rate policies, shifting from the previous unofficial target of zero for price stability in the first 99 years of the Fed's existence. Additionally, former central banker Neil Grossman provided historical context, mentioning that the 2% inflation idea originated with the Reserve Bank of New Zealand in 1995 and was later adopted by other central banks. Grossman implied that the Fed has utilized this target to overlook certain aspects of their monetary policy approach. To read the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Wondershare Filmora launches "Can You Feel the Beat?" music challenge on mobile app, featured on Google Play's homepage.

The platform aims to empower users worldwide to produce distinctive video content.
